Program Duration and Grading System - CSE at JKKNIU
The B.Sc. (Engg.) program in Computer Science and Engineering is a four-year degree divided into eight semesters, with a total requirement of 165 credit hours. Each academic year consists of two regular semesters (Semester I and Semester II), and the entire program must be completed within a maximum of six consecutive academic years. A standard semester spans 19 weeks, which includes 14 weeks for classes, a 2-week recess before final examinations, and 3 weeks for the semester final examinations. Credits are assigned based on the workload: one hour of theoretical lecture per week is equivalent to 1.0 credit (14 total lecture hours), while one laboratory session of two hours per week is equivalent to 1.0 credit (28 total lab hours).

Fourth Year Second Semester Syllabus - CSE at JKKNIU

Credit Distribution Summary - CSE at JKKNIU
The B.Sc. (Engg.) degree in Computer Science and Engineering requires the successful completion of 165.00 total credits. The majority of the curriculum is dedicated to core CSE (Departmental) courses, which account for 118.50 credits (71.81%). The remaining credits are distributed among foundational and interdisciplinary subjects: Mathematics and General Education (GED) each contribute 15.0 credits (9.10%), followed by Electrical and Electronic Engineering (EEE) with 13.50 credits (8.18%), and Physics making up the final 3.00 credits (1.81%) of the program requirements.

Program Duration and Grading System - CSE at JKKNIU
The B.Sc. (Engg.) program in Computer Science and Engineering is a four-year degree divided into eight semesters, with a total requirement of 165 credit hours. Each academic year consists of two regular semesters (Semester I and Semester II), and the entire program must be completed within a maximum of six consecutive academic years. A standard semester spans 19 weeks, which includes 14 weeks for classes, a 2-week recess before final examinations, and 3 weeks for the semester final examinations. Campus Life, Events & Administration

What clubs and festivals are active on campus?
Various clubs such as the JKKNIU Student Development Club (SDC) and programming or hobbyist groups exist. Major annual events include the cultural festival "Kuyasha Utshab" and celebrations for Nazrul Jayanti, reflecting the university's diverse cultural environment.

What residential halls and sports facilities are available?
The campus offers four residential halls named Agnibeena, Doloncapa, Bidrohi, and Shiulimala. There are sports grounds where students regularly participate in inter-university competitions.

What security and student support services are provided?
Located on a rural campus of approximately 57 acres, JKKNIU maintains standard security measures including proctors and university help-lines to ensure student safety and support.

Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) Location
The campus of Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) is situated at Namapara Battala in Trishal, Mymensingh. It is strategically located approximately 22 km from the Mymensingh city center and just 3 km away from the Dhaka-Mymensingh Highway. For those traveling from the capital, the university is roughly 100 km away from Dhaka. The specific location, "Battala," is named after a legendary and historic banyan tree that has stood for many years at Namapara, serving as a local landmark for the campus.
Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) Faculties Overview

Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) Infrastructure - Residential Halls
The university provides residential facilities for its students through four major halls. These halls are named after iconic works or themes related to the National Poet, reflecting the university's cultural identity: Agnibeena Hall, Doloncapa Hall, Bidrohi Hall, and Shiulimala Hall. These residences serve as the primary living spaces for students coming from various parts of the country.

Institutes and Quality Assurance at JKKNIU
In addition to its core faculties, the university features the Institute of Nazrul Studies, dedicated to researching and preserving the works and legacy of the National Poet Kazi Nazrul Islam. To maintain and enhance educational standards, the university also operates an Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC), which ensures that academic and administrative processes meet rigorous quality benchmarks.

How qualified are the teachers at JKKNIU?
The university employs over 160 full-time faculty members, including Professors, Associate Professors, Assistant Professors, and Lecturers, ensuring a strong academic environment.

What are the best departments at JKKNIU, and what is the status of the CSE department?
According to EduRank, JKKNIU is recognized for its publications in Computer Science and Environmental Science. The Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) was one of the first four established departments and benefits from modern academic and laboratory facilities.

How many faculties and departments does JKKNIU have, and what are the GPA criteria for admission?
JKKNIU has six faculties encompassing 25 departments across Science & Engineering, Arts, Business, Social Sciences, Fine Arts, and Law. Minimum GPA requirements for admission vary by discipline; applicants should refer to the annual admission circular available on the GST portal.

Do the departments have labs and research support?
Science and Engineering departments are well-equipped with laboratories. The university library is digitized and supports research activities with ICT tools such as Koha and DSpace.

Library & IT Support

What library services does JKKNIU provide?
The library offers automated services for acquisition, circulation, and reference management through Koha and DSpace digital repositories. It houses over 32,000 books, subscribes to 53 journals, and provides cyber facilities for users.

What digital support is available for students and faculty?
Free access to computing resources and internet is provided across campus. Facilities for photocopying and scanning are also available.

How can I get IT help or reset my password?
A central IT support desk operates within the university. Password resets and technical assistance are typically managed through the Registrar's or ICT office, with ticket logging systems in place.

What is the current student enrollment and faculty size?
Approximately 300 students are enrolled in the department, supported by a faculty of 15 members and 7 administrative staff.

Who are some of the key faculty members?
Professors include Dr. A. H. M. Kamal, Dr. Md. Mijanur Rahman, Dr. Md. Sujan Ali (Head of Department), among others. The department also has Associate Professors, Assistant Professors, and a Principal Demonstrator specializing in hardware.

What labs and research facilities are available?
The department features Software, Network, and Hardware labs. Additionally, the Emerging Technologies Lab (EmTech JKKNIU) supports research in Structural Health Monitoring, Robotics, Machine Learning, and the Internet of Things (IoT).

What are the main academic and research focus areas?
Research areas include Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Digital Signal Processing, Digital Image Processing, Medical Imaging, Computer Networks, and Network Security.

Is there an admission test, and where can past questions be found?
Yes, an admission test is required for all units under the GST system. Past questions may be accessed through senior student groups on social media platforms like Facebook or via preparatory coaching centers.

What are the application deadlines and how are results published?
Application deadlines vary each year and are announced in the official admission circular on the GST portal. Admission test results are posted online and displayed on notice boards at the university.

Faculties & Research

How many faculties and departments are there?
JKKNIU consists of six faculties and twenty-five departments, all operational and offering various academic programs.

What is the newest department and is it fully functional?

The historical development of Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) began with an initiative by the Executive Committee of the National Economic Commission (ECNEC) in February 2004, leading to the laying of its foundation stone by Prime Minister Khaleda Zia on March 1, 2005. The university was officially inaugurated on May 25, 2007, and while it was originally envisioned as the first culture-based university in Bangladesh, the University Act of 2006 established it as a general university with a specific emphasis on liberal arts education. Under the leadership of its first Vice-Chancellor, Mohammad Shamsur Rahman, the academic program commenced on June 3, 2007, with an initial batch of 185 students across four foundational departments: Bengali Language & Literature, English Language & Literature, Music, and Computer Science and Engineering. It is the first university established to preserve the memory of National Poet Kazi Nazrul Islam.

Program Duration and Grading System - CSE at JKKNIU
The B.Sc. (Engg.) program in Computer Science and Engineering is a four-year degree divided into eight semesters, with a total requirement of 165 credit hours. Each academic year consists of two regular semesters (Semester I and Semester II), and the entire program must be completed within a maximum of six consecutive academic years. A standard semester spans 19 weeks, which includes 14 weeks for classes, a 2-week recess before final examinations, and 3 weeks for the semester final examinations. In the years following its inception, JKKNIU underwent rapid expansion. In 2008, the Faculty of Business Administration was introduced with the Department of Accounting and Information Systems, and the Faculty of Social Science was established with the Department of Economics. Expansion continued in the 2009–2010 academic year with the addition of the Department of Finance and Banking and the Department of Fine Arts. By the 2010–2011 academic year, the university further diversified its academic offerings by establishing the Departments of Dramatics, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Public Administration, and Human Resource Management across its existing faculties.

Jatiya Kabi Kazi Nazrul Islam University (JKKNIU) Infrastructure - Residential Halls
The university provides residential facilities for its students through four major halls. These halls are named after iconic works or themes related to the National Poet, reflecting the university's cultural identity: Agnibeena Hall, Doloncapa Hall, Bidrohi Hall, and Shiulimala Hall. These residences serve as the primary living spaces for students coming from various parts of the country.

Institutes and Quality Assurance at JKKNIU
In addition to its core faculties, the university features the Institute of Nazrul Studies, dedicated to researching and preserving the works and legacy of the National Poet Kazi Nazrul Islam.
